Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now let me identify the key anomalies and categorize them 1 Skipped Activities Activities that should have occurred but did not 2 Out of Order Activities Activities performed in wrong sequence 3 Resource Role Mismatches Resources performing activities outside their role 4 Time Anomalies Unusual timing between activities 5 Policy Violations Activities that violate business rules

Name: Validation - PE
Text: All resources seem to be in their proper roles so department mismatch is not an issue here

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 The attempted early Y tag suggests an intentional bypass perhaps a priority customer or a system glitch allowing early shipment Could be a system permission issue or a manager overriding the standard process

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 The late confirmation Y suggests the confirmation was performed out of order possibly due to a backlog or a separate workflow Could indicate that the confirmation was forgotten in the original sequence and added after the fact or that the system allowed parallel processing

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Case 1004 The most severe payment received before fulfillment This could be A data entry error timestamps wrong A fraudulent transaction A system bypass A pre payment for an order that has not been processed yet shipment scheduled N suggests an issue with the shipment possibly due to out of sequence processing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SQL Queries to investigate 1 Find all cases where the normal process flow is violated 2 Identify activities that occurred out of order 3 Find cases where activities were skipped 4 Investigate timing patterns gaps between activities 5 Look at resource activity patterns for example resources handling out of order activities 6 Cross reference with order type priority orders might have different rules

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Query 1 Find cases with out of order activities Using a window function to check if activities are in expected order Query 2 Find cases with missing activities skipped steps For each case check if all expected activities are present Query 3 Calculate time gaps between activities Look for unusually short or long gaps Query 4 Find payment before shipment Critical anomaly check Query 5 Find shipment before credit check Critical anomaly check Query 6 Find shipment before stock validation Critical anomaly check Query 7 Aggregate by case to see activity sequences Use array agg or string agg to see the sequence per case Query 8 Look at order type correlations See if priority orders have more anomalies Query 9 Find resources involved in multiple anomalies Query 10 Find additional info patterns related to anomalies

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
